items.requirement: The accounts must state:
the part of the United Kingdom in which the micro-entity is registered;
the micro-entity’s registered number;
whether the micro-entity is a public or private company and whether it is limited by shares or guarantee;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the address of the micro-entity’s registered office;
where appropriate, the fact that the micro-entity is being wound up;
the name of the reporting entity and any change in its name since the end of the preceding reporting period;
the date of the end of the reporting period and the period covered by the financial statements;
the presentation currency; and
the level of rounding, if any, used in presenting amounts in the financial statements.
A complete set of financial statements of a micro-entity shall include the following:
a statement of financial position as at the reporting date with notes included at the foot of the statements; and
an income statement for the reporting period.
A micro-entity may use titles for the financial statements other than those used in FRS 105 as long as they are not misleading.
[Guidance]' Table of Equivalence for Company Law terminology'

Per the above, the standard allows for the usage of other terminology with reference to the core financial statements as long as it is clear what is meant. The standard also includes a table of equivalence between company law terms which may be more familiar to users of the accounts (such as "balance sheet" or "profit and loss account") and the wording used by the standard which may assist preparers or reviewers of the accounts.
Prepare accounts in accordance with the prescribed format headings as set out below:
references: R-C Sch 1.9A Section C, R-L Sch 1.9A Section C,

items.requirement: Statement of financial position format 1.
Called up share capital not paid;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: Fixed assets;
Current assets;
Prepayments and accrued income;
Creditors: amounts falling due within one year;
Net current assets / (liabilities);
Total assets less current liabilities;
Creditors: amounts falling due after more than one year;
Provisions for liabilities;
Accruals and deferred income;
Capital and reserves.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: Loans and other debt due to members
items.applicability_hint: N/A for companies — this disclosure is required for LLPs only.

items.requirement: Members' other interests
items.applicability_hint: N/A for companies — this disclosure is required for LLPs only.

items.requirement: Statement of financial position format 2.
Assets
Called up share capital not paid;
Fixed assets;
Current assets;
Prepayments and accrued income;
Capital, Reserves and Liabilities
Capital and reserves.
Provisions for liabilities;
Creditors
Amounts falling due within one year
Amounts falling due after one year
Accruals and deferred income;
[Guidance] Income Statement
Income Statement.
Turnover;
Other income;
Cost of raw materials and consumables;
Staff costs;
Depreciation and other amounts written off assets;
Other charges;
Tax;
Profit or loss.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: Profit or loss for the financial year before members' remuneration and profit shares
items.applicability_hint: N/A for companies — this disclosure is required for LLPs only.

items.requirement: In respect of every item shown in the entity’s financial statements the corresponding amount for the accounting period immediately preceding that to which the financial statements relate shall also be shown.
Where a corresponding amount is not comparable with the amount to be shown for the item in question in respect of the accounting period to which the balance sheet or profit and loss account relate:
the former amount shall be adjusted; and
particulars of the adjustment and the reasons for it shall be disclosed in a note to the financial statements.

There is no requirement for a company qualifying as a micro-entity to prepare a Directors' report and therefore a disclosure checklist is not included in this product. 

If a Directors' report is prepared then the checklist for small companies (Navigate UK GAAP Accounting FRS 102 1A disclosure checklist) should be used for guidance.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: Does the accountant’s report on the financial statements of the micro-entity include:
a title identifying the persons to whom the report is addressed (usually the Board of Directors/Members) and including the words ‘Chartered Accountant’s /Accountants’ Report to.............';

a statement that, in order to assist the directors/members to fulfil their duties under the Companies Act 2006, the accountants have prepared for the directors'/members' approval the financial statements which comprise [either: state the primary financial statements such as the Profit and Loss Account and the Balance Sheet] [ or: as set out on pages x-x] from the company's/LLP's accounting records and information and explanations supplied by the client;

a statement that the accountant is a practising member/ member firm of the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales and is subject to its ethical and other professional requirements which are detailed at icaew.com/membershandbook;

Optional paragraph. A statement that the report is made to the Company’s Board of Directors/Members as a body in accordance with the terms of engagement. An explanation as to the work carried out being in accordance with the requirements of ICAEW guidance and the purpose of the work and that, to the fullest extent permitted by law, no responsibility will be accepted for the work or the report to anyone other than the Company/LLP and the Company’s Board of Directors/Members, as a body;

Optional paragraph. A statement that the directors/members have acknowledged their duty to ensure that the company has kept adequate accounting records and to prepare statutory accounts that give a true and fair view of the assets, liabilities, financial position and profit or loss of the company/LLP and that they consider that the company/LLP is exempt from the statutory audit requirement for the year;

Optional paragraph. A statement that the accountants have not been instructed to carry out an audit or a review of the accounts of the company/LLP and that for this reason, they have not verified the accuracy or completeness of the accounting records or information and explanations given to them and they do not, therefore, express any opinion on the statutory accounts;
the name and signature of the accountant and any appropriate designation (but not ‘Registered Auditor’/ 'Statutory Auditor'), and
the date of the report.
Do the financial statements contain a reference to the fact that they are unaudited either on the front cover or on each page of the financial statements?

Details of advances and credits granted by a micro-entity to its directors and guarantees of any kind entered into by a micro-entity on behalf of its directors must be shown in the notes to the financial statements.
The details required of an advance or credit are:-
its amount;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: an indication of the interest rate;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: its main conditions;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: any amounts repaid;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: any amounts written off, and
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: any amounts waived.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: also disclose in the notes:
the total of amounts stated in (a)(i)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the total of amounts stated in (a)(iv)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the total of amounts stated in (a)(v)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the total of amounts stated in (a)(vi)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: guarantees of any kind entered into by the company on behalf of its directors, must be shown in the notes to its individual accounts. For each guarantee disclose:-
its main terms;
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the amount of the maximum liability that may be incurred by the micro-entity; and
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: any amount paid and any liability incurred by the micro-entity for the purpose of fulfilling the guarantee (including any loss incurred by reason of enforcement of the guarantee).
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: also disclose in the notes:
the total of amounts stated in (c)(ii)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the total of amounts stated in (c)(iii)
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: Disclose, in a note at the foot of the balance sheet, the total amount of each of the following that are not included in the balance sheet:
financial commitments;
guarantees;
contingencies

For any of the following that are not included in the balance sheet, disclose, at the foot of the balance sheet, an indication of the nature and form of any valuable security given by the company for each class of the following:
financial commitments;
guarantees;
contingencies

Disclose, at the foot of the balance sheet, the total amount of any financial commitments included above concerning pensions, in particular, when a micro entity participates in a defined benefit multi-employer plan, include a description of the extent to which it can be liable to the plan for other entities' obligations under the terms and conditions of the multi-employer plan.
Disclose separately, at the foot of the balance sheet, the total amount of any financial commitments included above which are undertaken on behalf of or for the benefit of:
any parent undertaking, fellow subsidiary undertaking or any subsidiary undertaking of the micro-entity, or
any undertaking in which the micro entity has a participating interest.
Disclose the average number of persons employed by the micro-entity in the reporting period.

If the entity is or has been party to arrangements that are not reflected in its statement of financial position and at the reporting date the risks or benefits arising from those arrangements are material, the nature and business purpose of the arrangements must be given in the notes to the financial statements to the extent necessary for enabling the financial position of the micro-entity to be assessed.
An entity shall disclose on the balance sheet:

the date when the financial statements were approved by the board of directors/all the members; and

NB. Though not required by legislation or FRS 105, it is considered best practice.

the name of the director/designated member who signed on behalf of the board and, if a copy of the accounts is sent to Companies House, that copy must include the signature of that director/designated member (unless submitted electronically).

The statement of financial position must contain, in a prominent position above the signature of the directors/members, a statement that the financial statements are prepared in accordance with the micro-entity provisions of the Companies Act 2006 or, for LLPs, Regulation 12 of the Limited Liability Partnerships (Accounts and Audit) (Application of Companies Act 2006) Regulations 2008 (SI 2008/1911).

If the entity is taking advantage of the exemption from an audit available under s477 (small companies/LLPs) or s480 (dormant companies/LLPs), the balance sheet must contain a statement by the directors/members, above the director's/designated member's signature to the effect that, for the year in question:
the entity was entitled to exemption from audit under s477 (or s480 as appropriate);
the members have not required the company to obtain an audit of its accounts for the year in accordance with s476; and
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the directors acknowledge their responsibilities for complying with the requirements of the Companies Act 2006 with respect to accounting records and the preparation of accounts.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for LLPs — this disclosure is required for companies only.

items.requirement: the designated members acknowledge their responsibilities for complying with the requirements of the Companies Act 2006 (as applied to limited liability partnerships by the Limited Liability Partnerships (Accounts and Audit) (Application of Companies Act 2006) Regulations 2008, with respect to accounting records and the preparation of accounts.
items.applicability_hint: N/A for companies — this disclosure is required for LLPs only.

items.requirement:
Note. The requirements for accounting records  and preparation of accounts are set out in s.386 and ss.393-414 respectively. However, there is no requirement to elaborate on these requirements or refer to the section numbers in the balance sheet statement.

The wording in (a) to (d) above follows the Companies House suggested wording.

If the micro-entity includes additional information in the notes to the financial statements to the micro-entity minimum accounting items, include the disclosure required by Section 1A Small Entities of FRS 102 that relates to that information
(see the Navigate UK GAAP Accounting FRS 102 1A disclosure checklist).

If the entity has opted to take advantage of some or all of the Companies House filing exemptions as permitted by the Companies Act 2006 s.444 (sometimes referred to as 'filleted' accounts), complete Navigate UK GAAP Accounting Small Company FRS 102 1A Filleted disclosure checklist.

[Guidance]
The Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 (ECCTA 2023) was enacted on 26 October 2023. Through this, the UK Government is reforming how companies report to Companies House and what information they report upon.

A key change is that small and micro companies will no longer have the option to prepare and file abridged or filleted financial statements, streamlining the types of financial statements filed at Companies House.

This change is anticipated to be coming into effect post 1 April 2027, and from this date micro entities will be required to file a copy of balance sheet, auditor’s report (unless exempt) and profit and loss account. A 21 months transition period will be provided to the companies before the option to file abridged or filleted accounts filing option is removed.
